quiz_questions.question_text: If u = <4, -2> and v = <-3, 5>, what is u - v?
quiz_questions.explanation: u - v = <4-(-3), -2-5> = <7, -7>. Vector subtraction subtracts corresponding components.

quiz_questions.question_text: Find 2u - 3v if u = <1, 4> and v = <2, -1>.
What is the magnitude of 3<-4, 3>?
If u = 3i - 2j and v = -i + 5j, find u + v in component form.
A vector v has magnitude 12 and direction angle 225°. Express v in component form.
quiz_questions.explanation: v_x = 12cos(225°) = 12(-sqrt(2)/2) = -6sqrt(2) and v_y = 12sin(225°) = 12(-sqrt(2)/2) = -6sqrt(2). Both components are negative since 225° is in Quadrant III.

quiz_questions.question_text: Find the vector from point P(2, -1) to point Q(-3, 4).
quiz_questions.explanation: PQ = Q - P = <-3-2, 4-(-1)> = <-5, 5>. The vector points from the initial point P to the terminal point Q.

quiz_questions.question_text: If |v| = 7 and v points in the direction of the unit vector <3/5, -4/5>, what is v?
quiz_questions.explanation: v = |v| * unit vector = 7 * <3/5, -4/5> = <21/5, -28/5>. The vector has magnitude 7 in the specified direction.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is the opposite (negative) of the vector <-3, 7>?
quiz_questions.explanation: The opposite of v = <a, b> is -v = <-a, -b>. So the opposite of <-3, 7> is <3, -7>. It has the same magnitude but points in the opposite direction.

quiz_questions.question_text: Vectors u and v have magnitudes |u| = 6 and |v| = 10 with an angle of 60° between them. Using the law of cosines, find |u + v|.
Simplify: (u + v) + w where u = <1, 2>, v = <3, -1>, w = <-2, 4>.
quiz_questions.explanation: (u + v) + w = <1+3, 2+(-1)> + <-2, 4> = <4, 1> + <-2, 4> = <2, 5>. Vector addition is associative, so the grouping does not affect the result.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is scalar multiplication of a vector?

options.text: Adding a number to each component of the vector
Multiplying each component of the vector by a real number, which scales the magnitude and may reverse direction
Multiplying two vectors together to get a scalar
Dividing the vector by its magnitude

quiz_questions.explanation: Scalar multiplication means multiplying a vector by a real number (scalar). If c > 0, the vector is scaled in the same direction; if c < 0, the direction is reversed. The magnitude becomes |c| times the original magnitude.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does it mean for vectors to be parallel?

options.text: They are perpendicular to each other
One is a scalar multiple of the other (same or opposite direction)
They share the same initial point

quiz_questions.explanation: Two vectors are parallel if one is a scalar multiple of the other: v = cu for some scalar c. If c > 0, they point in the same direction; if c < 0, they point in opposite directions.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is the triangle inequality for vectors?

options.text: |u + v| <= |u| + |v|, meaning the magnitude of the sum is at most the sum of the magnitudes

quiz_questions.explanation: The triangle inequality states |u + v| <= |u| + |v|. Equality holds only when u and v point in exactly the same direction. This mirrors the geometric fact that any side of a triangle is shorter than the sum of the other two sides.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is a linear combination of vectors?

options.text: The dot product of two vectors
A sum of scalar multiples of vectors, such as c1*v1 + c2*v2
A vector divided by a scalar
Two vectors placed end to end

quiz_questions.explanation: A linear combination of vectors v1, v2, ..., vn is an expression c1*v1 + c2*v2 + ... + cn*vn where c1, c2, ..., cn are scalars. Any vector in 2D can be written as a linear combination of two non-parallel vectors.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is vector subtraction?

options.text: Adding the magnitudes and subtracting the directions
Adding the first vector to the negative (opposite) of the second vector: u - v = u + (-v)
Taking the absolute difference of components

quiz_questions.explanation: Vector subtraction u - v is defined as adding u to the negative of v: u - v = u + (-v). In component form, <a1-a2, b1-b2>. Geometrically, u - v points from the tip of v to the tip of u when both start at the same point.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is the commutative property of vector addition?

options.text: u + v = v + u (order does not matter)

quiz_questions.explanation: The commutative property states that u + v = v + u for all vectors u and v. The order of addition does not affect the result. This is shown geometrically by the parallelogram law.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does it mean for a set of vectors to be linearly independent?

options.text: All vectors have the same magnitude
No vector in the set can be written as a linear combination of the others
All vectors are perpendicular to each other
The vectors all point in the same direction

quiz_questions.explanation: Vectors are linearly independent if no vector can be expressed as a linear combination of the others, or equivalently, the only solution to c1*v1 + c2*v2 + ... = 0 is c1 = c2 = ... = 0. In 2D, two non-parallel vectors are linearly independent.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is the associative property of vector addition?

options.text: (u + v) + w = u + (v + w) (grouping does not matter)

quiz_questions.explanation: The associative property states that (u + v) + w = u + (v + w). When adding three or more vectors, the grouping (which two you add first) does not change the final result.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is the distributive property of scalar multiplication over vector addition?
quiz_questions.explanation: The distributive property states c(u + v) = cu + cv. Scalar multiplication distributes over vector addition, just as multiplication distributes over addition for real numbers.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is the tip-to-tail method of vector addition?

options.text: Place vectors side by side and measure their combined width
Place the tail of the second vector at the tip of the first; the resultant goes from the tail of the first to the tip of the last
Reverse both vectors and add their magnitudes

quiz_questions.explanation: The tip-to-tail (head-to-tail) method places vectors sequentially so each new vector's tail starts at the previous vector's tip. The resultant vector goes from the tail of the first vector to the tip of the last.

quiz_questions.question_text: A boat travels at 15 km/h on a heading of 040° (measured clockwise from north). A current pushes the boat at 3 km/h on a heading of 130°. What is the boat's resultant speed (magnitude of the resultant velocity)?
quiz_questions.explanation: Converting headings to standard angles: 040° heading = 50° from east, 130° heading = -40° from east. Boat: <15cos(50°), 15sin(50°)> ≈ <9.64, 11.49>. Current: <3cos(-40°), 3sin(-40°)> ≈ <2.30, -1.93>. Sum ≈ <11.94, 9.56>. |R| = sqrt(142.6 + 91.4) ≈ sqrt(234) ≈ 15.3 km/h.

quiz_questions.question_text: An engineer needs to find the equilibrant force for two forces: F1 = <500, 0> N and F2 = <0, 300> N. The equilibrant is the negative of the resultant. What is the equilibrant?
quiz_questions.explanation: The resultant is F1 + F2 = <500, 300> N. The equilibrant is the negative of the resultant: <-500, -300> N. This force would balance the system, putting it in equilibrium.

quiz_questions.question_text: Three forces act on an object: F1 = <10, 0>, F2 = <-3, 8>, and F3 = <-7, -8>. What is the net force?
quiz_questions.explanation: Net force = F1 + F2 + F3 = <10+(-3)+(-7), 0+8+(-8)> = <0, 0>. The net force is zero, meaning the object is in equilibrium.

quiz_questions.question_text: A sled is pulled with a force of 50 N along a rope that makes a 25° angle with the ground. What horizontal force component moves the sled forward?
quiz_questions.explanation: The horizontal component is F_x = 50 cos(25°) ≈ 50(0.9063) ≈ 45.3 N. This is the portion of the force that actually moves the sled along the ground.

quiz_questions.question_text: A package hangs from two cables. Cable A has tension T_A at 50° above horizontal pulling left, and cable B has tension T_B at 35° above horizontal pulling right. If the package weighs 200 N, find T_A. (Use T_A cos(50°) = T_B cos(35°) and T_A sin(50°) + T_B sin(35°) = 200)
A plane flies at a velocity of v_plane = <200, 100> km/h relative to the air. A wind blows with velocity v_wind = <-50, 30> km/h. What is the ground speed (magnitude of the resultant)?
A football is thrown with an initial velocity of 25 m/s at 35° above horizontal. Find the initial velocity vector in component form.
A construction crane lifts a steel beam. Two cables pull with forces F1 = 1000<cos(70°), sin(70°)> and F2 = 1000<cos(110°), sin(110°)>. What is the total upward (y-component) force?
quiz_questions.explanation: y-components: 1000 sin(70°) + 1000 sin(110°). Since sin(110°) = sin(70°), total y = 2000 sin(70°) ≈ 2000(0.9397) ≈ 1879.4 N. The horizontal components cancel since cos(110°) = -cos(70°).

quiz_questions.question_text: A robot moves in three steps: d1 = <2, 3>, d2 = <-1, 4>, d3 = <5, -2>. If it needs to return to the origin in one move, what displacement vector d4 is needed?
quiz_questions.explanation: Total displacement so far: <2+(-1)+5, 3+4+(-2)> = <6, 5>. To return to origin: d4 = -<6, 5> = <-6, -5>.

quiz_questions.question_text: A surveyor walks from base camp to three landmarks: 500 m at 30°, then 700 m at 150°, then 400 m at 270°. What is the magnitude of the total displacement from base camp?
